---
title: "Getting Limewire to work"
date: 2008-06-21
forum: General Help
---

### Post by BGFG on 2008-06-21
I just got my limewire to work on Hardy Heron 8.04, Thought i'd jot it down and share. Hope someone else benefits.

Download :
[http://www.limewire.com/download/dow...sion=linux_deb](http://www.limewire.com/download/dow...sion=linux_deb)

Double click on the .deb file, choose Install Package

Once installed check to see if limewire can connect,If you have problems with "starting connection" FIRST exit limewire then,
Open a Terminal then type the command:

cd .limewire

# On my machine the gnutella.net file (the source of my connection problem) did not exist. use this command :

sudo touch gnutella.net

#then

sudo gedit gnutella.net

#Paste the contents below into the file:



# Save and close the file. restart limewire. Hope all is well.
# Of course, if Frostwire is your thing then simply download the frostwire.deb:

[http://www.frostwire.com/?id=thanks&...i586.deb&from=](http://www.frostwire.com/?id=thanks&...i586.deb&from=)

# and change the first command to

cd .frostwire

Everything else remains the same.

This guide runs on the premise that you also have the needed Java runtime installed on you machine(I use the latest version), if not then:

sudo apt-get install sun-java6-jre

#OR

Open Synaptic Package Manager and find the sun-java6-jre package and install it.
